summary: A universal AI agent cockpit — a retro-pixel web UI that visualises, narrates, and lets you redirect any running AI agent. Zero-config when Claude Code is installed.

stories.title: Zero-config Claude connection
stories.as: a developer with Claude Code installed
stories.want: Agent Arcade to detect my Claude subscription automatically without entering any API key
stories.so_that: I can start using it immediately after cloning the repo
acceptance_criteria: Opening the Console shows 'Claude Sonnet 4.6' in the header with no error
Settings → Providers shows 'Anthropic Claude: Connected ✓'
No API key input field exists anywhere in the UI
Works on Windows via cmd.exe /c claude fallback

stories.title: Live agent canvas
stories.want: to see my AI agents as pixel-art sprites on a retro game canvas
stories.so_that: I can visually track what every agent is doing at a glance
acceptance_criteria: Canvas renders within 2s of page load
Each agent has a sprite, name label, and status bubble
Agents show 'Idle' state when not working
Speech bubbles update with task descriptions when agents are active

stories.title: Console chat with AI response
stories.want: to type a message in the Console and receive an AI response
stories.so_that: I can ask questions and direct agents through natural language
acceptance_criteria: Typing a message and pressing Ctrl+Enter sends it
Response streams back and appears in the chat panel
Message counter increments ('2 msgs')
Canvas agents display the response in speech bubbles
Cost counter updates after each response
Feed bar shows the completed task

stories.title: Agent intervention
stories.want: to click an agent in the roster and redirect it mid-task
stories.so_that: I can course-correct agents without killing them
acceptance_criteria: Clicking an agent in the AGENT ROSTER opens an intervention panel
Panel shows agent name, status, Pause and Stop buttons
REDIRECT THIS AGENT text input is present
Quick-direction chips ('Use JWT instead of sessions', etc.) are shown
'Send New Direction →' button is visible

stories.title: Goal Mode — multi-agent orchestration
stories.want: to describe a high-level goal and have it broken into agent tasks automatically
stories.so_that: complex projects get parallelised without manual task decomposition
acceptance_criteria: Clicking 'Goal Mode' tab shows a goal description input
Example prompts are displayed below the input
Header shows 'Supervised Multi-Agent Orchestration'
Auto / + New / ↓ Export controls are present

stories.title: Command palette
stories.want: to press Ctrl+K and get a searchable list of commands
stories.so_that: I can drive the tool entirely from the keyboard
acceptance_criteria: Ctrl+K opens the command palette overlay
Palette shows /fix, /explain, /test, /review, /opt, /docs commands
Palette is searchable / filterable
Pressing Escape closes it

stories.title: Theme switching
stories.want: to switch between 8 visual themes for the canvas
stories.so_that: the tool feels personal and fits my workspace vibe
acceptance_criteria: Clicking the palette icon in the toolbar opens the theme picker
All 8 themes listed: Office, War Room, Retro Arcade, Cyber Lab, Campus Ops, Deep Space Lab, Dungeon Terminal, Hacker Bunker
Selecting a theme applies it instantly — canvas colour and subtitle change
Currently active theme is highlighted in the picker

stories.title: Provider settings — status only, no key entry
stories.want: the Settings → Providers tab to show connection status without asking for keys
stories.so_that: there is no confusion about where to paste credentials
acceptance_criteria: No <input> fields exist in the Providers tab
Each provider shows 'Connected ✓' or 'Not detected' badge
Claude auto-detected via Claude Code shows green
OpenAI/Gemini/Ollama show env var hint when not detected
Header reads 'Providers are detected automatically — no API keys needed.'

stories.title: Debug / event feed overlay
stories.want: to toggle a live event feed without losing the canvas
stories.so_that: I can debug agent telemetry without navigating away
acceptance_criteria: Clicking the bug icon in the toolbar opens the debug panel
Panel overlays the bottom of the canvas — canvas top remains visible
Panel shows session status line (Status, Transport, Agents, Events)
Timestamped event log entries are displayed
Clicking the bug icon again closes the panel

stories.title: XP / cost gamification bar
stories.want: to see my XP level, achievement count, and session cost at the bottom
stories.so_that: I have a fun sense of progression and budget awareness
acceptance_criteria: Bottom bar always shows: Level N, XP progress bar, 🏆 N achievements, 💰 $X.XXXX cost
Cost counter increases after each AI response
XP counter increments as actions are performed

stories.title: WhatsApp integration setup
stories.want: to see clear instructions for connecting WhatsApp to control agents from my phone
stories.so_that: I can direct agents while away from my desk
acceptance_criteria: Settings → WhatsApp tab shows connection status
When not connected, shows 'Error' status with startup instructions
Instructions show correct bun run command and gateway URL
Panel explains QR code scan flow

stories.title: Windows CLI spawn compatibility
stories.as: a Windows user with Claude Code installed
stories.want: the gateway and web server to correctly invoke the claude CLI
stories.so_that: I don't get 'No Anthropic API key detected' errors despite having Claude Code
acceptance_criteria: gateway/src/index.ts uses cmd.exe /c claude on Windows
web/src/app/api/chat/route.ts uses cmd.exe /c claude on Windows
gateway/src/directive-bridge.ts uses cmd.exe /c claude on Windows
IS_WIN constant and claudeSpawnArgs helper present in gateway
No EINVAL errors spawning claude on Windows

known_issues.description: Debug panel covers ~50% of canvas height when open (not the full canvas as in the previous bug, but still partially obscuring)
known_issues.workaround: Close the debug panel when not needed; agents remain visible in the top portion

known_issues.description: WhatsApp tab shows 'Error' status even when the client simply hasn't been started — no distinction between 'not started' and 'crashed'
known_issues.workaround: Follow the startup instructions shown in the panel

known_issues.description: Ollama listed as auto-detected in ProviderSettings but gateway only checks it in /v1/chat/providers, not exposed in the web /api/chat GET fallback
known_issues.workaround: Ollama works end-to-end via gateway; direct web fallback path doesn't enumerate it

flows.flow: Console open — no API key error
End-to-end Console chat → AI response → canvas bubbles
